title: Causal split between preprocessing and fold-dependent encoders
summary: Separate deterministic feature engineering from fold-dependent target encodings and model fitting so that leakage checks remain simple and encodings remain valid.
rationale: The attached run's preprocessing guard correctly caught target access, but this also means any future target encoding must be handled outside generic preprocessing. A clean pipeline boundary reduces leakage and implementation ambiguity.
implementation_hint: Stage one creates only target-free deterministic features for train and test. Stage two, inside each CV fold, fits target encoders and scalers on fold-training rows, transforms validation/test rows, and trains models. Stage three blends OOF predictions. Keep all stages row-indexed by original id.
expected_effect: Medium improvement in validation trust and experiment composability; AUC effect depends on whether fold-safe encodings are added.
risk: The pipeline is more complex than a single preprocessing function. Mistakes in test-time averaging of fold encoders can still create inconsistencies.
